revision.text:
'''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|和泉市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue ware pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * *{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=和泉市史第一巻|publisher=和泉市史編纂委員会|isbn=|pages=66-67}} * Kuze Hitoshi. "Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster" in *Izumi: Stories of Relics*, 2004, pp. 133-144.
== See Also == * {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|円墳" to "|円墳|1=Round Kofun"
revision.text:
'''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|和泉市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * *{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=和泉市史第一巻|publisher=和泉市史編纂委員会|isbn=|pages=66-67}} * Kuze Hitoshi. "Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster" in *Izumi: Stories of Relics*, 2004, pp. 133-144.
== See Also == * {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群}}

Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

'''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|和泉市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

'''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|和泉市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|和泉市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * *{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=和泉市史第一巻|publisher=和泉市史編纂委員会|isbn=|pages=66-67}} * Kuze Hitoshi. "Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster" in *Izumi: Stories of Relics*, 2004, pp. 133-144. == See Also == * {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群}}
<!--Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster--> <!--Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster--> <!--Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster--> <!--Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster-->

revision.text:
'''信太千塚古墳群'''（しのだせんづかこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[和泉市]]尾井町、王子町、小野町、黒鳥町、山荘町、伯太町一帯の丘陵上（信太山丘陵）に所在する[[古墳時代]]後期の[[群集墳]]である。
== 概要== 上記丘陵地帯に昭和38年（1963年）ごろには85基の古墳が確認されていたが、現在では多くの古墳が破壊、消滅している。[[前方後円墳]]は全長56メートルの1基のみで、大部分は[[円墳]]と考えられ、径10-20メートル程度のものが多く、次いで20-30メートルのものが多い。埋葬施設は、[[竪穴式石室]]、[[横穴式石室]]、[[木棺]]直葬、埴輪円筒棺と多種類であり、陶棺も2例数えられる。1つの古墳に副葬されている遺物は平均すると多くはないが、武器（刀・鏃）、農具（鎌）、馬具、装身具（金銀の箔を付けた耳輪や玉）さらには[[須恵器]]がある。この古墳群の中で発掘調査された代表的なものは78号墳がある。この古墳の横穴式石室の壁は主に和泉砂岩で構築されており、その中に[[和歌山県]][[紀ノ川]]流域に多い変成岩で造られた[[箱式石棺]]が配されていた。石棺の内外に少なくとも3体の埋葬が行われた痕跡があり、副葬品は金耳輪、銀耳輪、多数の須恵器があったという。その他に、この古墳群を特徴づけるものに、カマド塚の存在がある、カマド塚は木材で窯形の墓室を造り、外部を粘土で覆い、遺骸を入れてから火葬にし、同時に堅い墓室を焼き上げるという手法であり、当古墳群では2例調査されている。すでに多くの古墳が消滅しているものの、信太千塚古墳群は堺市[[陶器千塚古墳群]]とともに[[和泉国|泉州]]地方の代表的な古墳群であるといえる。
== 主要な古墳== 狐塚古墳は山荘配水池の横にある前方後円墳である。墳丘長56メートル、後円部径30メートル、同高さ4.5メートル、前方部幅38メートルをはかる。前方部の幅が広く、古墳時代後期の前半から中頃の築造が考えられる。66号墳は自衛隊信太山駐屯地内にある直径80メートルの大型の円墳である。周濠と方形の[[造り出し]]を持つとされているが現在埋め立てられており、わからなくなっている。中期から後期初頭の築造時期が考えられる<ref>久世(1968)pp.7-10</ref>。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
==参考文献 == *『和泉市史第一巻』 和泉市史編纂委員会 1965年 66-67頁 *久世仁士「信太千塚古墳群」『泉州の遺跡物語』2004年 133-144頁
== 関連項目== *[[陶器千塚古墳群]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのたせんつかこふんくん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:群集墳]] [[category:和泉市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[信太千塚古墳群]] to [[Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''信太千塚古墳群'''（しのだせんづかこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[和泉市]]尾井町、王子町、小野町、黒鳥町、山荘町、伯太町一帯の丘陵上（信太山丘陵）に所在する[[古墳時代]]後期の[[群集墳]]である。
== 概要== 上記丘陵地帯に昭和38年（1963年）ごろには85基の古墳が確認されていたが、現在では多くの古墳が破壊、消滅している。[[前方後円墳]]は全長56メートルの1基のみで、大部分は[[円墳]]と考えられ、径10-20メートル程度のものが多く、次いで20-30メートルのものが多い。埋葬施設は、[[竪穴式石室]]、[[横穴式石室]]、[[木棺]]直葬、埴輪円筒棺と多種類であり、陶棺も2例数えられる。1つの古墳に副葬されている遺物は平均すると多くはないが、武器（刀・鏃）、農具（鎌）、馬具、装身具（金銀の箔を付けた耳輪や玉）さらには[[須恵器]]がある。この古墳群の中で発掘調査された代表的なものは78号墳がある。この古墳の横穴式石室の壁は主に和泉砂岩で構築されており、その中に[[和歌山県]][[紀ノ川]]流域に多い変成岩で造られた[[箱式石棺]]が配されていた。石棺の内外に少なくとも3体の埋葬が行われた痕跡があり、副葬品は金耳輪、銀耳輪、多数の須恵器があったという。その他に、この古墳群を特徴づけるものに、カマド塚の存在がある、カマド塚は木材で窯形の墓室を造り、外部を粘土で覆い、遺骸を入れてから火葬にし、同時に堅い墓室を焼き上げるという手法であり、当古墳群では2例調査されている。すでに多くの古墳が消滅しているものの、信太千塚古墳群は堺市[[陶器千塚古墳群]]とともに[[和泉国|泉州]]地方の代表的な古墳群であるといえる。
== 主要な古墳== 狐塚古墳は山荘配水池の横にある前方後円墳である。墳丘長56メートル、後円部径30メートル、同高さ4.5メートル、前方部幅38メートルをはかる。前方部の幅が広く、古墳時代後期の前半から中頃の築造が考えられる。66号墳は自衛隊信太山駐屯地内にある直径80メートルの大型の円墳である。周濠と方形の[[造り出し]]を持つとされているが現在埋め立てられており、わからなくなっている。中期から後期初頭の築造時期が考えられる<ref>久世(1968)pp.7-10</ref>。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
==参考文献 == *『和泉市史第一巻』 和泉市史編纂委員会 1965年 66-67頁 *久世仁士「信太千塚古墳群」『泉州の遺跡物語』2004年 133-144頁
== 関連項目== *[[陶器千塚古墳群]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのたせんつかこふんくん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:群集墳]] [[category:和泉市の歴史]]

'''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|和泉市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin||qq=draft|qq=draft}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * *{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=和泉市史第一巻|publisher=和泉市史編纂委員会|isbn=|pages=66-67}} * Kuze Hitoshi. "Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster" in *Izumi: Stories of Relics*, 2004, pp. 133-144. == See Also == * {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{translated page|ja|信太千塚古墳群|version=102309836|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
'''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Town|qq=}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|ja|和泉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|zenpokoenfun|lt=zenpokoenfun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Province|qq=}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * *{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=和泉市史第一巻|publisher=和泉市史編纂委員会|isbn=|pages=66-67}} * Kuze Hitoshi. "Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster" in *Izumi: Stories of Relics*, 2004, pp. 133-144. == See Also == * {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|信太千塚古墳群|version=102309836|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Clustered tumuli]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]]
[[sn:Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster]]}}
[[sn:Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:信太千塚古墳群]]

Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Province|qq=}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11386019]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] cluster from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

<!--Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster--><!--Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster--><!--Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster--><!--Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster--> [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11386019]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] cluster from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type stone coffin)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the stone coffin, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11386019]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] cluster from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.

Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * *{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=和泉市史第一巻|publisher=和泉市史編纂委員会|isbn=|pages=66-67}} * Kuze Hitoshi. "Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster" in *Izumi: Stories of Relics*, 2004, pp. 133-144. == See Also == * {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|信太千塚古墳群|version=102309836|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11386019]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] cluster from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11386019]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] cluster from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in [[:en:Izumi, Osaka|Izumi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.

Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.
