Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] region.

The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11386019]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * *{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=和泉市史第一巻|publisher=和泉市史編纂委員会|isbn=|pages=66-67}} * Kuze Hitoshi. "Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster" in *Izumi: Stories of Relics*, 2004, pp. 133-144. == See Also == * {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|信太千塚古墳群|version=102309836|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]]

Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.

Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}} '''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka|1=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.

"Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster" in *Izumi: Stories of Relics*, 2004, pp. 133-144. == See Also == * {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信太千塚古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site}} '''Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster''' (信太千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} cluster from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} located in the hilly areas of {{ill|Oi Town|ja|尾井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Oji Town, Ono Town, Kurotori Town, Sanso Town, and Hakuta Town in {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|WD=Q696412|lt=Izumi, Osaka|1=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
By 1963 (Shōwa 38), 85 tumuli had been confirmed in the area, but many have since been destroyed or disappeared. The only {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} has a total length of 56 meters, while the majority are believed to be {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun (round tumuli)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, typically 10-20 meters in diameter, with some measuring 20-30 meters. Burial facilities include various types, such as {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, {{ill|yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, direct wooden coffin burials, and haniwa cylindrical coffins. Additionally, two ceramic coffins (陶棺) have been identified.
Artifacts buried as grave goods are generally sparse per tumulus but include weapons (swords and arrowheads), agricultural tools (sickles), horse trappings, ornaments (gold and silver-plated earrings and beads), and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. Among the excavated tumuli, the most representative is Kofun No. 78. The stone chamber of this horizontal tomb is constructed mainly from Izumi sandstone, housing a {{ill|hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of metamorphic rock common in the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1042624}} basin of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. At least three burials were conducted inside and outside the Sarcophagus, with grave goods including gold and silver earrings and numerous Sue pottery pieces.
A distinctive feature of this cluster is the presence of "kamado Kofuns" (カマド塚). These mounds employed a method in which a wooden kiln-like burial chamber was covered in clay, cremating the remains and simultaneously firing the chamber into a hardened state. Two such examples have been investigated within this cluster. Despite the significant loss of tumuli, the Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster, along with the {{ill|Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|陶器千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokai Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Sakai City, represents a quintessential kofun cluster in the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} region.
== Major Tumuli ==
The Kitsunezuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located beside the Sanso water distribution reservoir. It measures 56 meters in length, with the round portion measuring 30 meters in diameter and 4.5 meters in height, and the frontal portion 38 meters in width. Its wide frontal section suggests construction from the early to mid-late Kofun period.
Kofun No. 66, located within the Self-Defense Forces' Shinodayama base, is a large round tumulus with a diameter of 80 meters. It reportedly features a surrounding moat and a rectangular {{ill|Zōde|lt=protrusion|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōde|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}, though these elements are no longer visible due to landfill. It is estimated to have been constructed during the mid to early late Kofun period<ref>Kuze (1968), pp. 7-10</ref>.
